title: 顺水推舟
summary:
After Mrs. Montague leaves with a check for five hundred pounds, David confronts Rosaline about her decision to give the money away. David argues that Rosaline is wealthy due to the death of Gordon Clode, who died without a will, and warns her not to give any more money to the Claude family. He notes that Rosaline's sense of guilt is an inconvenience.

David then questions Rosaline about Lynn Marchmont's whereabouts, learning she went to Longleigh to see Rolly. Irritated by Lynn's relationship with Rolly, David intercepts her on a staircase halfway up the hill. He mocks Rolly and questions Lynn's upcoming June wedding. Lynn passionately asserts her love for Rolly, but David dismisses her feelings as an imagined ideal. During their tense encounter, David notices a woman crossing a door above Frobenk. Lynn identifies her as Francis.

David informs Lynn that her mother, Adela Marchmont, has already visited and received five hundred pounds. He further reveals that Aunt Kitty owes money to a medium and that the doctor has also come to them for funds. Distressed by the news, Lynn runs back toward the farm.

David enters the house through the French doors and finds Francis Claude speaking to Rosaline. Francis explains that Gordon's death has left Jeremy in a desperate financial situation and asks for assistance. She cites various reasons, including government regulations and mortgage loans, to justify a request for a million pounds. David, recognizing her storytelling ability, remains skeptical of her claims regarding Jeremy's economic bind.
